title: Fix return statements in PyMuPDFScraper
body:
Hi, just a quick fix for two bugs in `PyMuPDFScraper`:

1. In case of problems, there were no `return` statements in the except blocks, that caused the function to implicitly return `None`, and that errors out [in here](https://github.com/assafelovic/gpt-researcher/blob/master/gpt_researcher/scraper/scraper.py#L103C1-L113C22), because `None` can not be unpacked to three variables. I added returning of empty strings/lists, as is done in other scraper classes as well.

2. It was typed to return `str`, but actually it returns `tuple[str, list[str], str]`

Explore the source code to identify the files and functions to modify.

step_plan.summary: Ensure `PyMuPDFScraper.scrape()` always returns a consistent 3-tuple `(content, images, title)` by adding explicit returns in error handlers, and fix its return type annotation/docstring to match the actual tuple returned.

steps.operation: Fix `scrape` to always return a 3-tuple and correct its return type annotation/docstring
steps.reason: `scrape()` currently returns a 3-tuple on success but falls through in exception paths (implicit `None`), which breaks downstream tuple-unpacking. The type hint/docstring also incorrectly state it returns `str` rather than the actual `(content, images, title)` tuple.
steps.side_effects: Changes the public typing contract of `scrape()` to match real behavior; may require updating/appeasing type checkers in dependents. Runtime behavior on errors changes from returning `None` to returning safe defaults like `("", [], "")`, which may cause callers to proceed with empty values rather than failing fast; add/adjust tests that assert consistent tuple returns on timeout/exception cases.
